model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current micro-resnet is likely capacity-limited at 8 channels and a 32-unit head. Widening the stem and classifier is the simplest next test after the schedule-heavy tweak failed to improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is still relatively small, and depth is now wired into the network. One more residual block is the cheapest capacity increase to test next under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run is still improving as residual capacity increases, and the architecture remains very small. One more residual block is the cleanest next test before touching optimization again.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is still small for CIFAR-10 even after adding depth, and the last gain was only marginal. A modest width increase is the simplest next capacity test and should be cheaper than changing the optimizer recipe again.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The widened/deeper model is now the main win, so the next lever is the optimization scale. A slightly smaller one-cycle peak LR is a low-risk way to tighten final convergence without changing the architecture.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 112","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The lower learning rate underperformed, so restore the stronger optimization scale while testing a modest width bump. This keeps the current residual stack intact and probes whether the model still has room to benefit from extra capacity without a large compute jump.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 5","old":"DEPTH = 4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 112","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last width bump regressed, so revert to the best-performing width and spend the remaining capacity budget on an extra residual block. This keeps the successful optimization recipe intact while testing whether the model is still depth-limited.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 6","old":"DEPTH = 5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Depth is still the only knob that has been improving this configuration, and the model remains small. One more residual block is the cleanest way to test whether the stack still has room to benefit without changing the optimization recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The residual stack looks close to saturation, so the cheapest remaining lever is batch geometry. A larger batch can stabilize AdamW updates and improve the fixed-step fit without changing the model or schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Batch size was the strongest non-architecture lever so far, and 128 improved validation loss materially. A moderate step to 192 tests whether the gain continues without jumping all the way to an aggressive full-batch regime."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size has been the most reliable source of improvement so far, and 192 was not obviously saturating. Pushing to 256 is the next simple test to see whether the fixed-step regime still benefits from larger, stabler updates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Batch size has continued to be the most productive low-complexity lever, and 256 still left room for improvement. The next simplest test is a further batch increase to see whether the larger-update regime keeps helping under the fixed-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 7","old":"DEPTH = 6","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The depth axis has been consistently helpful, while the batch-size gains are starting to taper. One more residual block is the cheapest remaining architectural test and should add little risk relative to another batch jump.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The structural gains have largely flattened out, so the remaining room is in the optimizer scale. A modest LR reduction is the safest way to see if the current large-batch setup can settle to a slightly better minimum.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The 320 batch and lower LR regressed relative to the last best run. Reverting both to the previously strongest stable settings is the most defensible next step before trying any new knob."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current configuration is already well-stabilized by the large batch and deeper residual stack, so the next plausible gain is to back off regularization a bit. Lower weight decay is the smallest test of whether the model is still being held back by excess shrinkage."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The very low weight decay underperformed, but the previous 5e-4 setting may be a bit stiff for this larger-batch run. An intermediate value is the most sensible next regularization test before changing the model again."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The lower weight decay was a regression relative to the best-known run. The safest final move is to restore the regularization level that already produced the strongest verified loss under this training setup."}
